khan_exercises.title: Meet the adjective
khan_exercises.description: Identify adjectives and the nouns they describe.

items.question_content:
Which word does **the bolded adjective** describe?

>I drank from a **silver** goblet.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Adjectives describe nouns (people, places, things, or ideas). They typically come *before* the noun they describe.
In this sentence, the adjective "silver" describes the **"goblet"**.

hints: Adjectives describe nouns (people, places, things, or ideas). They typically come *before* the noun they describe.
In this sentence, the adjective "enormous" describes the noun **"tent"**.

khan_exercises.title: Meet the article
khan_exercises.description: Identify articles and the words they describe.

items.question_content:
**Which word in the sentence is an article?**

>The guitar was missing strings.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Articles are a type of adjectives that tells you whether or not something is specific.

_The_, _a_, and _an_ are the only articles in English.
The only article in this sentence is **"the."**

items.question_content:
**Which word in the sentence is an article?**

>Reading is a fine hobby.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Articles are a type of adjectives that tells you whether or not something is specific.

The only article in this sentence is **"a."**

khan_exercises.title: Choosing between definite and indefinite articles
topic_path: Choosing between definite and indefinite articles

items.question_content:
Choose the appropriate definite or indefinite article!

>I have never seen \_\_\_\_\_\_ elephant up close.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
"The" is the definite article, used when discussing a specific thing.

"A/an" are the indefinite articles, used when discussing something that's not specific.

The indefinite article changes depending on the word that follows it.  When the word begins with a consonant sound (like `b` or `g`), you’d say ​_a_​; when the word begins with a vowel sound (like `a` or `o`), you’d say ​_an_​.

Be careful with words that begin with vowels, but start with consonant sounds, like "use" or "union". Those take the article *"a"*.

Also be careful with words that begin with consonants, but start with vowel sounds, like "honor" or "hour". Those take the article *"an"*.

Since we're not talking about a specific elephant, we should use an indefinite article.

Since "elephant" starts with a vowel sound, we should use **"an"**.

items.question_content:
Choose the appropriate definite or indefinite article!

>\_\_\_\_\_\_ person next to me in line is named Stewart.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
"The" is the definite article, used when discussing a specific thing.

"A/an" are the indefinite articles, used when discussing something that's not specific.
Since we're talking about a specific person, we should use the definite article **"the"**.

Also be careful with words that begin with consonants, but start with vowel sounds, like "honor" or "hour". Those take the article *"an"*.

Since we're not talking about a specific adventure, we should use an indefinite article.

Since "wild adventure" starts with a consonant sound, we should use **"a"**.

Be careful with words that begin with vowels, but start with consonant sounds, like "use" or "union". Those take the article *"a"*.

Also be careful with words that begin with consonants, but start with vowel sounds, like "honor" or "hour". Those take the article *"an"*.

Since we're not talking about a specific feast, we should use an indefinite article.

Since "delicious feast" starts with a consonant sound, we should use **"a"**.

items.question_content:
Choose the appropriate definite or indefinite article!

>Keblor’s Books is \_\_\_\_\_\_ only bookstore in town.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
"The" is the definite article, used when discussing a specific thing.

"A/an" are the indefinite articles, used when discussing something that's not specific.
Since we're talking about a specific bookstore, we should use the definite article **"the"**.
